evals.prompt: Create an Issuer Brief for Apple Inc.
evals.expected_output: Single fenced ```html code block containing a complete standalone HTML document built from the Issuer Brief template. Target = Apple Inc. with 3 credit peers from getEntityPeers, all 27 pib-* placeholders populated, 19x8 financial table mostly filled (with '--' allowed), 5 valuation tables, peers/rating/KI tables, strategy and segments lists, risk + rating + ESG tables, monthly price and debt-maturity SVG charts, and a populated sources list.
assertions: Output is a complete standalone HTML document (doctype + </html>)
Report uses the skill's template scaffold (core pib-* table IDs present)
All 27 core pib-* placeholders defined in the document
6 narrative sections (overview, industry, mgmt, credit, risks, esg) all populated
Financial table >= 100/152 cells populated (19 rows x 8 year cols, allowing '--' placeholders)
Peers comparison table has >= 3 <tr> rows
Peers rating table has >= 3 <tr> rows
Rating history table has >= 1 <tr> row
Risk table has >= 3 <tr> rows
Strategy list (#pib-strategy-list) has >= 3 <li> entries
Segments list (#pib-segments-list) has >= 2 <li> entries
Share price monthly chart and credit chart both contain <svg>
ESG table (E/S/G) all 3 rows populated (focus + score)
Sources section contains >= 5 entries

evals.prompt: I need a full company report for Tesla Inc. — an Issuer Brief covering everything from financials to ESG
evals.expected_output: Single fenced ```html code block with the full Issuer Brief HTML for Tesla + 3 peers. Same structural requirements as eval-1 (all 27 pib-* placeholders, 5 valuation tables, peers/rating/KI tables, monthly + credit SVGs, E/S/G scores, and >= 5 sources), tested via a natural-language phrasing.
assertions: Output is a complete standalone HTML document (doctype + </html>)
Report uses the skill's template scaffold (core pib-* table IDs present)
All 27 core pib-* placeholders defined in the document
6 narrative sections (overview, industry, mgmt, credit, risks, esg) all populated
Financial table >= 100/152 cells populated (19 rows x 8 year cols, allowing '--' placeholders)
Peers comparison table has >= 3 <tr> rows
Peers rating table has >= 3 <tr> rows
Rating history table has >= 1 <tr> row
Risk table has >= 3 <tr> rows
Strategy list (#pib-strategy-list) has >= 3 <li> entries
Segments list (#pib-segments-list) has >= 2 <li> entries
Share price monthly chart and credit chart both contain <svg>
ESG table (E/S/G) all 3 rows populated (focus + score)
Sources section contains >= 5 entries
